text_excerpt:
INDICATION:  ___ year old man with polysubstance abuse admitted w/ multiple rib
fractures subsequently developing hypoxemic resp failure requiring intubation.
Treated briefly for PNA. Also w/ new a-fib w/ RVR and some signs of volume
overload.// Eval for volume or consolidation

TECHNIQUE:  Chest PA and lateral

COMPARISON:  ___

IMPRESSION: 

Bilateral effusions right greater than left are stable.  Patchy parenchymal
opacities bilaterally right greater than left are also unchanged.  There are
multiple displaced right-sided rib fractures, unchanged.  Cardiomediastinal
silhouette is stable.  No pneumothorax is seen.

EXAMINATION:  CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with ETT// eval for interval change      eval for
interval change

IMPRESSION: 

Comparison to ___.  The patient has been extubated.  The lung
volumes have decreased.  Bilateral parenchymal opacities are slightly denser
than on the previous image, the image resembles the 1 taken on ___, at 02:02.  No larger pleural effusions.  Normal size of the heart.

EXAMINATION:  Chest single view

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with ETT advanced  2 cm// eval ETT position

TECHNIQUE:  Chest portable AP

COMPARISON:  ___, 10:47, about 45 minutes ago

FINDINGS: 

ET tube has been advanced and now terminates about 2 cm above the bifurcation
of the trachea NG tube is below the diaphragm.  Bilateral lung opacities
unchanged

IMPRESSION: 

Tip of ET tube 2 cm above bifurcation of the trachea

EXAMINATION:  Chest single view

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with et tube// et tube placement

TECHNIQUE:  Chest portable AP

COMPARISON:  ___ 1 hour prior

FINDINGS: 

Tiny right apical pneumothorax persist.  An ET tube has been placed
terminating 4.5 cm above the bifurcation of the trachea.  NG tube is below the
diaphragm.  No change to bilateral opacities.

IMPRESSION: 

Tiny right apical pneumothorax, and bilateral opacities with no interval
change.  Placement of ET tube and NG tube.

EXAMINATION:  Chest single view

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with flail chest// acute resp distress/ SOB

TECHNIQUE:  Chest portable AP

COMPARISON:  ___ 02:02

FINDINGS: 

Right chest tube is out.  There is persistent minute right apical
pneumothorax.  Small right effusion.  Small subcutaneous emphysema on the
right.  Bilateral pulmonary opacities are seen in the more severe on the right
the which is slightly worse than of the previous exam obtained 7 hours
previously.  Also increased is a left lung opacity particularly in the left
midlung field.  There acute changes likely present the pulmonary edema
Bilateral lower lobe atelectasis as previously.

IMPRESSION: 

My new right apical pneumothorax.  Interval increase in bilateral lung
opacities likely due to edema.
